text.title: Proposal From Discovery Template — Examples
text.summary: One worked end-to-end example: discovery notes from a SaaS payments engagement converted into a sent proposal.

section.title: Discovery notes (input)
section.code:
# Acme Corp — discovery call 2026-05-21
Attendees: Anna (freelancer), Marek (Acme CTO), Eva (Acme PM)
## Pain - Checkout p95 latency 1.4s (baseline 600ms); started after Stripe SDK migration in April. - Stripe retry rate climbed from 6% → 18% in 6 weeks; ~$8k/week revenue leak. - Internal team focused on a new feature; no bandwidth for retry-tuning until Q4.
## Budget signal - Marek: "we have $25-50k in Q3 budget; outcome-based offers welcome".
## Evidence - Call recording: https://drive.acme.com/calls/2026-05-21-discovery.md - Stripe dashboard: https://stripe.com/dashboard/payments?period=last30

section.title: Filled proposal (output)
section.code:
--- version: 1.0.0 owner: Anna Schmidt last_reviewed: 2026-05-23 client: Acme Corp title: Acme — payment-funnel rebuild proposal ---
## Pain (from discovery) Checkout p95 latency 1.4s (target ≤600ms); Stripe retry rate 18% (target ≤6%); estimated weekly revenue leak ~$8k. Evidence: call recording + Stripe dashboard (links below).
## Options
### Option 1 — Light (fixed) - Scope: Stripe retry tuning + checkout-path latency budget instrumentation. - Price: $6,500; duration: 2 weeks.
### Option 2 — Standard (fixed) - Scope: Light + p95 latency rebuild + new retry policy roll-out. - Price: $18,000; duration: 6 weeks.
### Option 3 — Outcome-based - Scope: Standard with success fee on retry-rate ≤6% by week 8. - Price: $12,000 base + $6,000 success fee; duration: 6 weeks.
## Evidence - https://drive.acme.com/calls/2026-05-21-discovery.md - https://stripe.com/dashboard/payments?period=last30
## Actions - 2026-05-26 — Anna sends PDF to procurement contact. - 2026-06-02 — Acme PM confirms chosen option in writing.

section.title: Outcome review (next iteration)
section.p: At the close-rate review date (2026-06-09), record which option Acme picked (or none), the time-to-yes, and any pushback. Feed the result into the v1.1.0 review of the template — if outcome-based wins, surface that pattern; if it never wins, deprecate the option.
